title: quercus canescens var. urticifolia
intro: Quercus canescens var. urticifolia, also known as the nettle-leaf white oak, is a variety of white oak tree native to North America. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Quercus canescens var. urticifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being, and to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems.
